phases.result:
Planning pipeline complete. Story #1 is live and `agent::ready`.

## What I did

Ran **only** the `/plan` pipeline headlessly (interrogate → author → critics → dry-run → persist), auto-proceeding both HITL gates under `--yes`.

**Interrogate** produced a `full`-route envelope (seed 93 words), no duplicate Stories, and a verified-empty codebase snapshot — the working tree carries no application source, only the Mandrel scaffold.

**Authored one cohesive Story** (default-single policy — this is a single coherent capability, no architectural seam to split on):

> **#1 — Issue and list credit notes against orders** → [issue](https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-23-brownfield-longitudinal-mandrel-47a00acd/issues/1)

The 8 acceptance criteria encode every rule from the change request as observable HTTP/report behavior (auth 401, issued-only guard, the `outstanding = total − payments − existing credit notes` cap, cumulative capping across successive notes, input validation, and the receivables reduction), with unit + contract + validate verify commands.

**Key planning decision:** because the tree is empty at plan time, I recorded `Current state (verified 2026-07-23): …` in the Spec and kept every `changes[]` entry as `creates` — I deliberately avoided phantom `exists` references to a non-present order/payment/receivables domain (those would hard-fail the file-assumption gate). The Spec flags `changes[]` as advisory so the executor integrates with (refactors) that domain if it already exists at delivery time, or establishes the minimal surface if not.

**Gates:** both critics returned `dispatch: false`, text-hygiene clean; dry-run passed all validators; real persist created the issue and flipped it to the terminal `agent::ready`.

Next step (a separate session — I did **not** deliver): `/deliver 1`.

Story #1 delivered and landed. Here's the outcome:

## `/deliver 1 --yes` — complete

**Story #1 "Issue and list credit notes against orders" → `landed`**

- Resolved the id set: single Story, no dependency edges. No separate Epic issue exists — the plan emitted one `type::story` (the default N=1), so there was no Epic to discover; delivering the Story is the whole plan.
- Dispatched it through the standard role-scoped `story-worker` path, which ran `deliver-story` end-to-end.

**What landed** (branch `story-1` → PR #2 → squash-merged to `main`, commit `abc8da3`):
- `src/domain/orders.js` — order model (total, issued state, payments, customer)
- `src/domain/credit-notes.js` — issuance rules: `issued`-only guard, `outstanding = total − payments − existing notes` cap (cumulative), 400-class input validation vs 422-class rule violations
- `src/domain/receivables.js` — receivables rollup net of issued credit notes
- `src/store/credit-notes-store.js` — in-memory store
- `src/http/auth.js` — signed-in-user guard seam (checked before any body read/persist)
- `src/routes/credit-notes.js` — `POST`/`GET /orders/:orderId/credit-notes`
- 3 test files (domain, receivables, route contract) — 22 tests covering AC-1…AC-8

The repo had no application source at plan time, so the worker established the minimal coherent orders/payments/receivables/auth surface needed to make credit notes self-verifiable, per the Spec's "where it is absent, establish the minimal surface" instruction.

**Gates:** validation, base-sync, and code review all passed (0 critical/high/medium; 1 suggestion). `npm run typecheck` clean, `npm test` 22/22 green. Auto-merge armed and merged; Story flipped to `agent::done`.

**One benign note:** the pre-existing dirty `temp/` in the main checkout blocked the post-land *local* fast-forward of `main` (`baseFastForward: false`). This does not demote the land — `origin/main` carries the squash-merge; a later `git pull` reconciles the local checkout.
